items.question: Which formula is Bayes' theorem?
items.explanation: Bayes' theorem reverses conditioning using prior P(A), likelihood P(B|A), and evidence P(B).

items.question: Disease prevalence is 0.1%, sensitivity 99%, specificity 95%. What is P(disease | positive) approximately?
P(B) = 0 in your model. What is P(A|B)?
items.explanation: Conditional probability requires P(B) > 0; division by zero makes P(A|B) undefined.

items.question: How do you compute P(B) when A and Aᶜ partition the space?
items.explanation: Law of total probability sums weighted likelihoods over the partition.

items.question: A fair deck: P(King | face card) equals?
